answer: Blocking 'Functionality cookies' may impact the user experience if some or all of the services provided by the website or third-party providers do not function properly.

Analytics cookies are used to count visits and traffic sources to measure and improve the site's performance, while Advertising cookies are used to build a profile of the user's interests and show relevant adverts on other sites.

If a user opts out of 'Advertising cookies,' they will experience less targeted advertising. This differs from opting out of 'Analytics cookies,' where the outcome is that the site will not know when the user has visited, and will not be able to monitor its performance.
